summary: E2E testing of the NEW 'Design Requests - New' module (cloned from Marketing/Design Requests). Backend: 11/11 pytest passed — create returns DRN-YYYY-NNNN, SM auto-seeds as 'Design Requests - New Lifecycle (default)' with initial state 'submitted', transition + comment + CSV export all work, file upload/download works, /api/roles exposes module_labels['design_requests_new']='Design Requests - New' + module_categories['Marketing'] includes 'design_requests_new', shared masters (marketing-request-types + master-departments) are reused (no duplicates), and data isolation from the old marketing_requests collection is verified in both directions. Frontend: /design-requests-new list+Kanban renders with header 'Design Requests - New', shows all 8 SM states, sidebar has 'Design Requests - New' item, New Request navigates to /design-requests-new/new, form renders all 7 shared types (Neck Tags, Request Bottle Design Concept, Request Physical Sample, Standees, Video, Presentation, Others) with department 'Design' pre-selected, submit creates DRN-2026-0001 and navigates to /design-requests-new/{id}, detail page shows lifecycle name + status history + action buttons; clicking 'Start Working' transitioned Submitted→In Progress; delete request works. Cleanup performed (test request deleted).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/design_requests_new.py is ~1500 lines — a near-verbatim clone of routes/marketing_requests.py. Consider extracting the shared machinery (upload/download/file-attach, list/counts/export query builder, transition engine wrapper, comment/version/production helpers) into a shared module (e.g. utils/design_requests_common.py) parameterised by (collection_name, files_collection, workflow_key, request_prefix). Right now every future bug fix has to be applied in both files — high maintenance risk.

Duplicated docstring/header text: design_requests_new.py's module docstring still says 'Marketing Requests — State-Machine-driven lifecycle router' and mentions prefix '/marketing-requests'. Update to reflect the new module for clarity.

In design_requests_new.py, functions still reference 'marketing_request' semantics (e.g. Slack event_type='marketing_request_created', 'marketing_request_urgent', 'marketing_request_status_changed', 'marketing_request_commented', link path '/marketing-requests/{request_id}' inside notify_users, path segment 'nyla-crm/{tenant_id}/marketing-requests/{file_id}/…'). The event_type/link discrepancies mean in-app notifications will deep-link to the OLD module page. Recommend using distinct event types (e.g. 'design_request_new_created') and link '/design-requests-new/{request_id}'.

Copy-pasted RequestComment kind label — 'Removed attachment …' and 'Request … created' messages are fine, but 'Estimated finish date set to …' etc still appear in status_history for the new module. Verify these still make sense in the new context (they do — just calling out).

_can_delete_request / _can_edit_request check permissions on the key 'design_requests_new' (correct), but the sibling functions in marketing_requests.py check 'marketing_requests'. Confirm the frontend Role Management screen renders BOTH toggles independently — verified via GET /api/roles module_labels which does include both.

SM key 'design_requests_new' shares FIELD_REGISTRY entry with 'marketing_requests' (sm_helpers.py: FIELD_REGISTRY['design_requests_new'] = FIELD_REGISTRY['marketing_requests']). Fine for parity but any future divergence needs to be a shallow copy, not aliased reference, otherwise mutations bleed across modules.
test_credentials: CEO surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (login POST /api/auth/login returns session_token, passed as Authorization: Bearer <session_token>).
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted a single TEST_DRN_NEW request per pytest test case (module-scoped fixture cleans up), plus one UI-driven DRN-2026-0001 request that was deleted via the UI Delete Request button. No residual test data — verified on the list page which shows '0 requests' after cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows for the new module PASS. Reusable pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_design_requests_new.py. Frontend testids on new module: design-requests-new-page (list), new-mr-page (form), mr-type-chip-{id}, mr-dept-select, mr-due-input, mr-details-input, mr-submit-btn, mr-detail-page (detail), action-{key}-btn (SM transitions), mr-comment-input/send-btn, mr-delete-request-btn. Kanban has all 8 states (submitted, inputs_needed, in_progress, in_review, approved_internal, final_approved, production_in_progress, production_completed). Numbering confirmed independent: DRN-YYYY-NNNN vs MR-YYYY-NNNN. Post-run list count = 0 requests.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — new-feature validation, no bugs found.
